summary.para: Base abstract class that provides some common functionality for providers seeking to integrate with management assisted build.
Override of base IPreprocessBuildWithReport.

returns.para: The callback order.

summary.para: Override of base IPreprocessBuildWithReport.

returns.para: String that specifies the key to be used to set or get settings in EditorBuildSettings.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.AdaptivePerformanceBuildHelper_1.OnPostprocessBuild(UnityEditor.Build.Reporting.BuildReport)

summary.para: Override of base IPostprocessBuildWithReport.

member.param: BuildReport instance passed in from build pipeline.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.AdaptivePerformanceBuildHelper_1.SettingsForBuildTargetGroup(UnityEditor.BuildTargetGroup)

summary.para: Helper function to return current settings for a specific build target.

member.param: An enum that specifies which platform group this build is for.

returns.para: A Unity object that represents the settings instance data for that build target, or null if not found.

summary.para: Helper utilities for build-time modifications.
Add the custom scaler in users' assets to the provider settings. Only needed if you want to scan for custom scalers during build time.
Get value for boost mode on startup for given settings

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.AdaptivePerformanceBuildUtils.UpdateBootConfigBoostSetting(System.String,System.String,System.String)

summary.para: Add key to boot.config

member.param: Player build path
Key to add or update
Value for the key

summary.para: Container class that holds general settings for each build target group installed in Unity.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettingsPerBuildTarget.AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettingsForBuildTarget(UnityEditor.BuildTargetGroup)

summary.para: Given a build target, get the general settings container assigned to it.

member.param: An enum that specifies which platform group this build is for.

returns.para: The instance of AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettings assigned to the key, or null if no instance is assigned.

summary.para: Serialization override.
Serialization override.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettingsPerBuildTarget.SetSettingsForBuildTarget(UnityEditor.BuildTargetGroup,UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettings)

summary.para: Set specific settings for a given build target.

member.param: An enum that specifies which platform group this build is for.
An instance of AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettings to assign for the given key.

summary.para: Get specific settings for a given build target.

member.param: An enum that specifies which platform group this build is for.

returns.para: The instance of AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettings assigned to the key, or null if no instance is assigned.

summary.para: An interface for automating the initial setup of a provider package.
The loader full type name for this package
The loader type name for this package
Package initialization key
Package name property

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.AdaptivePerformancePackageInitializationBase.PopulateSettingsOnInitialization(UnityEngine.ScriptableObject)

summary.para: Initialize package settings

member.param: The scriptable object instance to initialize

returns.para: True if successful, false if not.

summary.para: The settings full type name for this package
The settings type name for this package
Build attribute to identify which platforms a loader supports.
String representation of https:docs.unity3d.comScriptReferenceBuildTargetGroup.html|UnityEditor.Build.BuildTargetGroup.

Array of BuildTargets, each of which is the representation of https:docs.unity3d.comScriptReferenceBuildTarget.html|UnityEditor.Build.BuildTarget aligned with buildTargetGroup.
Currently only advisory.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.AdaptivePerformanceSupportedBuildTargetAttribute.#ctor(UnityEditor.BuildTargetGroup)

summary.para: Constructor for attribute. We assume that all build targets for this group will be supported.

member.param: Build Target Group that will be supported.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.AdaptivePerformanceSupportedBuildTargetAttribute.#ctor(UnityEditor.BuildTargetGroup,UnityEditor.BuildTarget[])

summary.para: Constructor for attribute

member.param: Build Target Group that will be supported.
The set of build targets of Build Target Group that will be supported.

summary.para: Provide access to the metadata store. Currently only usable as a way to assign and remove loaders to or from an AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings instance.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.Metadata.AdaptivePerformancePackageMetadataStore.AssignLoader(UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings,System.String,UnityEditor.BuildTargetGroup)

summary.para: Assigns a loader of type loaderTypeName to the settings instance. Will instantiate an instance if one can't be found in the user's project folder before assigning it.

member.param: An instance of AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings to add the loader to.
The full type name for the loader instance to assign to settings.
The build target group being assigned to.

returns.para: True if assignment succeeds, false if not.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.Metadata.AdaptivePerformancePackageMetadataStore.RemoveLoader(UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings,System.String,UnityEditor.BuildTargetGroup)

summary.para:
Remove a previously assigned loader from settings. If the loader type is unknown or an instance of the loader can't be found in the project folder, no action is taken.
Removal will not delete the instance from the project folder.

member.param: An instance of AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings to add the loader to.
The full type name for the loader instance to remove from settings.
The build target group being removed from.

returns.para: True if removal succeeds, false if not.

summary.para: A container class that contains all provider setting objects used in build profile. This is to accommodate for addComponent API only takes a single object with a type, but we have multiple objects with the same type, and are thus aggregated under this container.
all provider setting objects used in build profile.
Provides an interface for describing specific loader metadata. Package authors should implement this interface for each loader they provide in their package.
The user-facing name for this loader. Will be used to populate the list in the Adaptive Performance Provider Management UI.

The full type name for this loader. This is used to allow management to find and create instances of supported loaders for your package.
When your package is first installed, the Adaptive Performance Provider Management system will use this information to create instances of your loaders in AssetsAdaptive PerformanceLoaders.

Defines the priority of the loader. The higher the number, the higher the priority. Basic provider will take 0, google provider takes 1, and samsung provider will take 2. If a user takes the already taken value, the order will not be guaranteed.

The full list of supported build targets for this loader. This allows the UI to only show the loaders appropriate for a specific build target.
Returning an empty list or a list containing just https:docs.unity3d.comScriptReferenceBuildTargetGroup.Unknown.html|BuildTargetGroup.Unknown will make this loader invisible in the UI.

Implement this interface to provide package-level information and actions.
Adaptive Performance Provider Management will reflect on all types in the project to find implementers of this interface. These instances are used to get information required to integrate your package with the Adaptive Performance Provider Management system.
Returns an instance of IAdaptivePerformancePackageMetadata. Information will be used to allow the Adaptive Performance Provider Management to provide settings and loaders through the settings UI.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Editor.Metadata.IAdaptivePerformancePackage.PopulateNewSettingsInstance(UnityEngine.ScriptableObject)

summary.para: Allows the package to configure new settings passed in.

member.param: ScriptableObject instance that represents an instance of the settings type provided by IAdaptivePerformancePackageMetadata.settingsType.

returns.para: True if the operation succeeded, false if not. If implementation is empty, just return true.

summary.para: Top-level package metadata interface. Create an instance of this interface to provide metadata information for your package.

Information about the status of being a default platform provider.
The flag is set by the package info to define the status of a provider on a specific build platform.

Information about the deprecation state of the package.
The flag is set to identify deprecated packages.

The full URL for the license for your package.
The full URL is used to inform the user that a separate package license exists. Must point to the same license as the one in the package.json file which displays in the provider list. The user accepts the license by installing the provider.
List of IAdaptivePerformanceLoaderMetadata instances describing the data about the loaders your package supports.
The package id used to track and install the package. Must be the same value as the name keyword in the package.json file, otherwise installation will not be possible.
User-facing package name. Should be the same as the value for the displayName keyword in the package.json file.

The full type name for the settings type for your package.
When your package is first installed, the Adaptive Performance Provider Management system will use this information to create an instance of your settings in AssetsAdaptive PerformanceSettings.
This is a custom Editor base for Provider Settings. It displays provider general settings and you can use it to extend provider settings editors to display custom provider settings.
Whether the development settings are collapsed or not.
Whether the indexer settings are collapsed or not.
Whether the runtime settings are collapsed or not.
Whether the scaler settings are collapsed or not.
Specify which platform the provider should be supported on.
Displays the base developer settings. Requires DisplayBaseSettingsBegin() to be called before and DisplayBaseSettingsEnd() after as serialization is not taken care of.
Displays the base indexer settings. Requires the serializedObject to be updated before and applied after as serialization is not taken care of.
Displays the base runtime settings. Requires DisplayBaseSettingsBegin() to be called before and DisplayBaseSettingsEnd() after as serialization is not taken care of.

Starts the display block of the base settings. Needs to be called if DisplayBaseRuntimeSettings() or DisplayBaseDeveloperSettings() gets called. Needs to be concluded by a call to DisplayBaseSettingsEnd(). Pass isLegacyAPI = false to hide the legacy warning banner and comply with new APIs. Default is true (for compatibility).

returns.para: False if the settings cannot be loaded. Otherwise true.

summary.para: Ends the display block of the base settings. Needs to be called if DisplayBaseSettingsBegin() is called. Pass isLegacyAPI = false to comply with new APIs in this class. Default is true (for compatibility).
Default UI for showing provider settings on both project settings and build profile.
Displays the base scaler settings. Requires the serializedObject to be updated before and applied after as serialization is not taken care of.
Display default common base settings for provider on specific target, which user could choose to override if they are using DisplayProviderSettings.
Controls whether or not the 'AutomaticGameModeEnabled' option is available. Default value is false.
Controls whether or not the 'AutomaticPerformanceModeEnabled' option is available. Default value is true.
Controls whether or not the 'EnableBoostOnStartup' option is available. Default value is true.
Controls whether or not the 'Indexer/Thermal Action Delay' option is available. Default value is false.
Enables Settings Editor and generates the reorderable list to store all profiles in.

Whether to show targetGroupSelection tab when using the default base setting. User should use this property to conditionally define their UI if they choose to custom the provider setting UI for each platform and uses the targetGroupSelection tab.
String to show when the provider is not available on this platform.

The subsystem is used for simulating Adaptive Performance in the Editor with the https:docs.unity3d.comManualDeviceSimulator.html|Device Simulator. It is also used for Adaptive Performance tests and to simulate Adaptive Performance when it is not available on the hardware you work with.
Required to simulate performance changes. To change AutomaticPerformanceControl, you have to set AcceptsPerformanceLevel to true. See PerformanceDataRecord.PerformanceLevelControlAvailable.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.ChangeFlags.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.CpuPerformanceBoost.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.CpuPerformanceLevel.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.GpuPerformanceBoost.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.GpuPerformanceLevel.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.CpuFrameTime.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.GpuFrameTime.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.OverallFrameTime.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.PerformanceMode.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Simulator.Editor.SimulatorAdaptivePerformanceSubsystem.SetClusterInfo(UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.ClusterInfo)

summary.para: Helper for the device simulator to change cluster info settings. Those settings are usually changed by a device directly.

member.param: New Cluster Info values.

summary.para: The Simulator Provider controls Subsystems needed to access Adaptive Performance features and the systems lifecycle.
Required to simulate performance changes. To change AutomaticPerformanceControl, you have to set AcceptsPerformanceLevel to true. See PerformanceDataRecord.PerformanceLevelControlAvailable.
Returns the application lifecycle.
Callback that is called when the application goes into a pause state.
Callback that is called when the application resumes after being paused.
Returns the capabilities of the provider.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.ChangeFlags.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.CpuPerformanceBoost.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.CpuPerformanceLevel.
Main constructor, used to initialize the provider capabilities.
Cleanup when the subsystem object is destroyed.
Enable the boost mode for the CPU.

returns.para: Returns if CPU boost mode was successfully enabled.

summary.para: Enable the boost mode for the GPU.

returns.para: Returns if GPU boost mode was successfully enabled.

summary.para: This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.GpuPerformanceBoost.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.GpuPerformanceLevel.
Returns the initialization status of the system.
See IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.MaxCpuPerformanceLevel.
See IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.MaxGpuPerformanceLevel.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.CpuFrameTime.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.GpuFrameTime.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.OverallFrameTime.
Returns the performance level control.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.PerformanceMode.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Simulator.Editor.SimulatorAdaptivePerformanceSubsystem.SimulatorProvider.SetClusterInfo(UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.ClusterInfo)

summary.para: Helper for the device simulator to change cluster info settings. Those settings are usually changed by a device directly.

member.param: New Cluster Info values.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.Simulator.Editor.SimulatorAdaptivePerformanceSubsystem.SimulatorProvider.SetPerformanceLevel(System.Int32&,System.Int32&)

summary.para: Set the performance level for both the CPU and GPU.

member.param: The CPU performance level to request.
The GPU performance level to request.

returns.para: Returns if the levels were successfully set.

summary.para: Perform initialization of the subsystem.
Returns the stats of the provider.
Stop running the subsystem.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.TemperatureLevel.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.TemperatureTrend.
Simulator subsystem try to initialize initializes successfully.

returns.para: true if initialized

summary.para: Update current results and flags.

returns.para: The latest PerformanceDataRecord object.

summary.para: The current version of the Device Simulator Adaptive Performance Subsystem. Matches the version of the Adaptive Performance Subsystem. See AdaptivePerformanceSubsystem.Version.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.WarningLevel.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.TemperatureLevel.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.TemperatureTrend.
This property is a wrapper around an internal PerformanceDataRecord object. For more details, see PerformanceDataRecord.WarningLevel.
Static constants for the Simulator Provider.
Key that Unity uses to store and retrieve custom configuration settings from EditorBuildSettings.
SimulatorProviderLoader implements the loader for the Adaptive Performance Device Simulator plugin.
Returns the currently active Simulator Subsystem instance, if any.
Implementation of AdaptivePerformanceLoader.Deinitialize.

returns.para: True if successfully deinitialized the Simulator subsystem, false otherwise.

summary.para: Implementation of AdaptivePerformanceLoader.GetDefaultSubsystem

returns.para: The Simulator as currently loaded default subststem. Adaptive Performance always initializes the first subsystem and uses it as a default, because only one subsystem can be present at a given time. You can change subsystem order in the Adaptive Performance Provider Settings.

summary.para: Implementation of AdaptivePerformanceLoader.GetSettings.

returns.para: Returns the Simulator settings.

summary.para: Implementation of AdaptivePerformanceLoader.Initialize.

returns.para: True if successfully initialized the Simulator subsystem, false otherwise.

summary.para: Returns if the provider loader was initialized successfully.
Returns if the provider loader is currently running.
Implementation of AdaptivePerformanceLoader.Start.

returns.para: True if successfully started the Simulator subsystem, false otherwise.

summary.para: Implementation of AdaptivePerformanceLoader.Stop.

returns.para: True if successfully stopped the Simulator subsystem, false otherwise

summary.para: Provider Settings for Simulator Provider which controls the editor runtime asset instance which stores the Settings.
Returns Samsung Provider Settings which are used by Adaptive Performance to apply Provider Settings.

returns.para: Samsung Provider Settings

summary.para: This is custom Editor for Simulator Provider Settings.
Override of Editor callback to display custom settings.
Shows the setting for simulator provider.
Usage Dial is a VisualElement to display a values with a color dial.
Constructor, setups the usage dial with its correct percentages.

member.name: M:UnityEditor.AdaptivePerformance.UI.Editor.UsageDial.SetThresholds(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)

summary.para: Change the percentage levels of the dial.

member.param: Force and intemediate update.

summary.para: If the usage dial should show the percentage label.
A value the usage dial should represent.
